title: 2025 12 19 Bigquery Lead Management System Handoff Document
html:
TL;DR

  • Technical handoff doc Yahir wrote for Elijah to operate a 6.4GB B2B leads CSV (MASTER_B2B_LEADS) inside Google BigQuery instead of crashing n8n / Excel / Sheets.
  • Architecture: local CSV → Google Cloud Storage staging → BigQuery table (b2b_leads.master_b2b_leads) → SQL filter → mark used=TRUE → n8n pulls and emails.
  • Cost analysis included: ~$0.13/mo storage for 6.4GB; queries effectively free under BigQuery's 1TB/month free tier; alternatives (pandas, Clay/Airtable, Sheets) were considered and ruled out.
  • Step-by-step status: Steps 1–4B complete (project, dataset, gcloud SDK, storage bucket), Step 4C in progress (1.4 MiB/s CSV upload, ~80min ETA). Steps 5–9 written as next-action playbook.
  • Use case driving this: extract 10,000 unused construction businesses, mark them used to prevent duplicate outreach, fire automated email campaigns via n8n.

Notes

This is the late-2025 lead-gen infrastructure for a Yahir-Elijah collaboration — the kind of "you bring the ops, I'll write the SQL" dynamic that recurs whenever Yahir is teaching a non-technical partner how to operate a data primitive. The ghost white label / ghost kitchen mental model applies: build the back-of-house once, drive multiple front-of-house campaigns through it.

The schema includes the all-important used BOOL DEFAULT FALSE column added via ALTER TABLE after load — atomic marking is the whole reason Yahir picked BigQuery over Sheets-based dedup gymnastics. Mentions Yahir hasn't yet confirmed which column name in the CSV holds business industry (industry vs category vs sic_code) — that's the first thing to inspect after load completes.

Connects to Yahir's broader email deliverability + outbound engine thinking. This is the supply side; rankett's sales script is the demand side. The BigQuery infrastructure could later feed leads into Rankett-targeted campaigns or sit underneath Elijah's own ventures.

Connections

  • elijah leach — the operator this is handed to
  • n8n — workflow surface that consumes BigQuery output
  • email deliverability — the downstream concern
  • rankett — adjacent demand-side product
  • ghost white label — the build-once, deploy-many pattern
